_comment: Qualitative layer: distilled, cited creator takes per spec — populated by the watch-creators skill from video transcripts. Claims are paraphrased (auto-captions are lossy); every take carries a timestamped deep link for verification. Takes NEVER feed tiers or consensus. superseded: true when a newer take from the same creator or a newer patch state replaces it. metaNotes[]: a SEPARATE lane for per-spec season/meta-OUTLOOK reads from the general PTR-news creators (generalCreators in community.json — e.g. izen). Same honesty rules (cited, timestamped, never feeds tiers) but validation requires the creator be a generalCreators entry, so news-lane generalists stay firewalled out of the specialist takes[]/consensus layers. sentiment: positive|negative|neutral|mixed. patchContext carries the content lens (raid vs M+ outlook); a spec may hold one of each.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: The baffling first-week nerfs that gutted Subtlety's burst resource generation have largely been rolled back this build: Shadow Techniques' base proc frequency is up 40% and it now generates 5 energy per proc (was 4), and Shadowcraft is reworked so Shadow Techniques trigger 25% more often during Shadow Dance and grant an extra combo point (its old bonus energy folded into baseline). The net effect restores the Season 1 feel of overcapping resources and flooding burst windows, so despite the back-and-forth he reads Subtlety as likely pretty strong for Season 2 barring bad tuning.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: Gormo's Bite is redesigned and relocated to the center of the tree (45s cooldown, 25 energy): it now cleaves the target plus two nearby enemies, applies a 14-second bleed, and repeats 10% of all finisher damage as pure shadow split among affected targets — turning a talent nobody used into genuine three-target cleave and extra armor-bypassing priority-target burst even in single target. Downsides: it costs a GCD, generates no combo points itself, and must be pressed on cooldown to stay synced with the 1.5-minute Shadow Blades. The capstone it displaces, new First Dance (extends Shadow Dance if you stay out of combat 6s), looks niche for PvP/M+ and odd as a capstone.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: Blizzard is trying to revive the ambush build for Outlaw — Audacity now grants ambush +60% damage and the reworked Hidden Opportunity reduces ambush's cost — but on current PTR tuning an ambush build does roughly 10% less than the best (or even mediocre) builds, and Hidden Opportunity is gated behind two points of Summarily Dispatched, competing with Fan the Hammer, Killing Spree, Keep It Rolling and Preparation. Until the effect is baked into Audacity or the pathing is loosened (or ambush's base damage is buffed), the build isn't worth playing.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: He rates the Outlaw tier set well — 2-set Dispatch +15%, 4-set giving Sinister Strike/Ambush a chance to make the next Dispatch free and hit as if at max combo points. This build's adjustment stops Sinister Strike's double-hit from each separately rolling that proc (now only the initial cast, nominally ~20%), which slightly narrows the gap between ambush and sinister-strike builds and fixes the free Dispatches to correctly interact with the apex/Fatebound (though Trickster stays the likely hero pick). Killing Spree was also tuned to less often hit the wrong target in AoE.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: Little new for Assassination this week beyond the strong early changes: the one tweak makes Implacable rank 3's King's Bane shank strikes properly feed combo-point-generator interactions like Lethality (crit) and Cold Blooded Killer, which he welcomes but finds odd didn't already work. He repeatedly flags that Assassination needs more dev follow-up and better communication than it has gotten.

takes.creator: AutomaticJak
takes.claim: Season 2's first patch-notes pass nerfs Mistweaver's Spinning Crane Kick damage and Way of the Crane healing transfer while buffing Jadefire Teachings; the tier set both boosts Rising Sun Kick's damage and its extra healing and (4-set) grants a chance for free extra RSK casts — a shift away from 'spin to win and forget,' though he notes you'll still spin at high target counts (players estimate around eight).

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: He reads 12.1 as deliberately weaning Elemental off Ascendance-window and overload RNG: the apex talent's overload amplification drops sharply (Ascendance overload bonus 75% to 30%, activation Lava Bursts at 50% value, rank bonuses trimmed from ~50% to ~30%), while redesigned Power of the Maelstrom (bankable 2-stack Lava Burst amp off Lightning Bolt/Chain Lightning casts) and Stormkeeper (instant, +100% no-overload Lightning Bolts vs overloading Chain Lightnings) push toward deliberate cast-combo gameplay.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: In exchange for the Ascendance/overload trims, Elemental's base kit gets large compensation buffs — Lava Burst and Lightning Bolt baseline damage up 30%, Chain Lightning and Flame Shock up 60%, plus 25% stronger self-healing (Healing Surge, Earth Shield, Healing Stream Totem) — which he sees as a genuine attempt to make the spec strong outside cooldown windows, cautioning that the burst still needs to feel satisfying for the playstyle to keep its fans.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: Enhancement's playstyle is built upon rather than redefined in 12.1: the new gameplay hook is the tier set (guaranteed primary-target Fire Nova eruptions every 2 seconds, 4-set Crash Lightning cooldown reduction with a stacking 8% amp that feeds the apex talent) layered on flat base-kit buffs (Lightning Bolt/Chain Lightning +20%, Lava Lash/Stormstrike/melee +15%), while RNG power is trimmed — Doom Winds' Windfury activation bonus halved and Stormbringer's Tempest plus Totemic's Surging Totem tuned down.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: Flags Restoration as the Shaman spec getting conspicuously little PTR attention: its only real 12.1 change is Healing Rain gaining a shorter cooldown with a longer duration so it can be re-placed sooner in raids (mainly a Farseer quality-of-life fix), and he relays community concern about Resto's raid standing carrying over from Season 1 given how much less attention it is receiving than the other two Shaman specs or other healers.

takes.creator: Kalamazi
takes.claim: The 06-30 build's Affliction 2-set redesign (Agony +15%, Corruption/Wither +25%) plus the Hellcaller buffs (Blackened Soul +45%, Wither +25%) meaningfully lift Hellcaller single-target funnel; his sims suggest this could push Hellcaller close to or slightly above Soul Harvester in single target, though Hellcaller's shard economy still feels worse.

takes.creator: Kalamazi
takes.claim: The Dark Harvest double-CDR bug (Cold Week + the 4-set Seed→Unstable Affliction interaction each granting cooldown reduction) that made spamming Seed of Corruption in pure single target absurdly strong has been fixed; he expects single-target Seed-spam won't remain worthwhile now that the bug is gone.

takes.creator: Kalamazi
takes.claim: Destruction feels very undertuned in raid testing; the shared Hellcaller buffs help a little but he questions whether it is enough, and no Destruction capstone or single-target-vs-AoE rework is coming this patch given the fast patch cadence (Mythic testing is the next build).

takes.creator: Azortharion
takes.claim: Azortharion reads 12.1 Beast Mastery as power shifted out of Bestial Wrath and cooldown windows into baseline abilities, producing a flatter, more forgiving spec (longer Beast Cleave) whose gameplay feel is basically unchanged — if you like BM now you still will, and vice versa.

takes.creator: Azortharion
takes.claim: He calls Marksmanship's 12.1 changes a very mixed bag he is genuinely conflicted about: the Explosive Shot redesign and the Black Arrow/Deathblow proc fix are promising, but reduced Aimed Shot emphasis flattens the profile and Bulletstorm nerfs hurt funnel damage, so the verdict depends on how Explosive Shot gameplay pans out.

takes.creator: Azortharion
takes.claim: He counts 12.1 as a win for Survival: Lethal Barbs lets you use any weapon at little to no DPS difference, talent repositioning opens new builds, and with no other meaningful changes the spec stays in an already-strong spot.

takes.creator: YoDaTV
takes.claim: The 12.1 PTR talent-tree rework leaves Protection Paladin in a bad spot: the bottom gate is now flooded with must-have talents (Sanctuary's 5% damage reduction and Relentless Inquisitor's 5% haste were moved down) without enough points to take them all, and pathing now forces previously never-taken nodes like Focused Enmity to reach key defensives.

takes.creator: AutomaticJak
takes.claim: Holy Paladin brings up the rear among healers for Season 2: heroic raid testing showed no big single-target healing moments for its Holy Light-heavy kit, the Innervate rework works out to a sizable mana nerf for its expensive casts, and passive-healing specs are winning raid spots instead.

takes.creator: AutomaticJak
takes.claim: Discipline's healing numbers were phenomenal in Season 2 raid testing, but with roughly 40-50% of its output coming just from applying Void Shields the spec has become so centralized on its apex talent that the rest of the kit gets lost, which he flags as a design head-scratcher despite the strong throughput.

takes.creator: AutomaticJak
takes.claim: Holy Priest lands in his Season 2 loser tier: it got no meaningful changes again, the new tier set is built around passively-procced Renews and grants haste (long one of the spec's worst stats), and its shortfall in passive healing keeps it on the bench in raids.

takes.creator: YoDaTV
takes.claim: Vengeance's 12.1 changes are small quality-of-life adjustments (Sigil of Chains baseline with Sigil of Silence as an opt-in), and while defensives stay RNG-dependent and pack gathering got worse, the spec still looks like the frontrunner meta tank for Season 2 — especially with Prot Paladin's rework in disarray.

takes.creator: YoDaTV
takes.claim: Blood DK's updated 12.1 tier set (Death Strike granting passive strength plus occasional Mirror Image procs) is a nice gameplay-changing bonus and its damage is good, but defensively little changes, so he doesn't see Blood becoming the top meta tank.

takes.creator: Supatease
takes.claim: Notes the 12.1 build's Shaman changes center on tier sets rather than Elemental, with the class falling in line with Blizzard's stated Season 2 goals of reduced CC and toned-down cooldown power.

takes.creator: Supatease
takes.claim: Reads the reworked Voltaic Blaze two-set, which makes Fire Nova hit the primary target for triple damage, as fixing the bonus losing effectiveness against small pulls and playing much better in PvP by shifting it toward single-target.

takes.creator: Supatease
takes.claim: Sees the Blackened Soul and Wither damage buffs plus the redesigned Affliction set bonus (Agony +15%, Corruption +25%) as a very large amount of DoT damage, questioning whether that much power belongs in a set bonus instead of the class baseline.

takes.creator: Supatease
takes.claim: Likes the Tactical Edge redesign that makes Colossus Smash immediately grant a Sudden Death use, agreeing it will be more rotationally impactful and open interactions for Slayer and execute-focused builds.

takes.creator: Supatease
takes.claim: Reacts positively to Protection's Revenge update bringing back the free-Revenge counterattack mechanic with an added consistency layer (dodges, parries, and autos can make the next Revenge cost no rage), alongside the Ravager bleed-amp also applying to Prot.

takes.creator: Kalamazi
takes.claim: Expects the 45% Blackened Soul and 25% Wither buffs combined with the redesigned Agony/Corruption two-set to be a significant single-target gain for Hellcaller, potentially pushing it very close to or slightly above Soul Harvester for Affliction.

takes.creator: Kalamazi
takes.claim: Says Destruction felt very undertuned in 12.1 raid testing and, while the Wither and Blackened Soul buffs help Hellcaller Destro somewhat, he doubts they are enough and expects no capstone or single-target-versus-AoE rework this late in the PTR cycle.

takes.creator: Kalamazi
takes.claim: Argues the new Affliction tier set only helps funnel scenarios and does not fix the spec's core single-target-versus-AoE talent split, so with the Dark Harvest cooldown-reduction double-dip bug now fixed, pure single-target Affliction still looks weak.

takes.creator: Kalamazi
takes.patchContext: 12.1 PTR raid testing (Heroic, The Venomous Abyss)
takes.claim: Affliction looks good on the tier's add-dense, seed-cleave fights (Xal'atath the Soul Coiler, Vashj'ir, The Twin Fangs), but the strength comes from spamming Seed-with-Sow onto stacked targets rather than strong single-target/UA output — it is weak on the pure patchwork boss (Cizzerak) and on the 2-target council fight (Entombed Sentinels).

takes.creator: Kalamazi
takes.patchContext: 12.1 PTR raid testing (Heroic, The Venomous Abyss)
takes.claim: Destruction is 'in a shambles' and 'needs serious help' this tier; the Entombed Sentinels permanent 2-target cleave fight is about the only spot he'd actually pick it.

takes.creator: Kalamazi
takes.patchContext: 12.1 PTR raid testing (Heroic, The Venomous Abyss)
takes.claim: Demonology is the most flexible and overall strongest Warlock spec in testing — his pick for best overall single target (Corrupted Altar) and strong on Diabolist cleave/council fights like Vashj'ir.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: The 12.1 Astral Power rework — stronger spenders but a nerfed Touch of the Cosmos free-proc chance — is making Balance feel slower and more filler-cast-heavy in early PTR impressions, and he hopes Blizzard monitors or partially rolls it back before the pacing makes the spec unfun.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: Feral is only getting number tuning in 12.1 (Saber Jaws and Chomp buffed, Ripper up 20%, popular picks trimmed) instead of the spec-tree rework players wanted, and cutting the apex talent's proc chance risks making the spec less proc-driven and less fun even though it has seen decent Mythic+ play.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: Guardian's old apex talent was so uncompetitive that Season 1 builds ignored it entirely, so the brand-new 12.1 guardian-spirit rework tied to Mangle, Maul and Thrash is a needed redesign — but rank 3 looks unfinished on the PTR and it will take weeks of testing to know if the new version is worth playing.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: Restoration gets the best quality-of-life pass of the Druid specs in 12.1 — mana cost cuts across core spells, a simpler self-contained Innervate, baseline defensive/knockback protection on Tranquility, and a Swiftmend/Everbloom rework — which he hopes makes Resto competitive again after Mistweaver lapped it in Season 1.

takes.creator: Obli
takes.claim: Frost DK looks overtuned on the Season 2 PTR: the shift to a dual-wield-dominated playstyle driven by the new tier set, plus major buffs to Icy Death Torrent and Remorseless Winter and stronger auto-attack damage, make it excellent in both single target and AoE.

takes.creator: Obli
takes.claim: He argues DK's PTR dominance is mostly the last-boss trinket (roughly 1,400 primary stat, far above other options) on top of an above-average class, expects it to be nerfed, and worries Blizzard will instead over-nerf Frost and Unholy themselves before 12.1 goes live.

takes.creator: Obli
takes.claim: Unholy is only slightly behind Frost on the PTR and not out of the meta in his view, but the reworked Soul Reaper now spending ghoul stacks and the 8-ghoul cap on Army of the Dead make the spec feel more rune-starved and slightly clunkier than in Season 1.

takes.creator: Pkpawner
takes.claim: After playing every Venomous Abyss PTR boss, Pkpawner thinks the raid broadly suits Windwalker — heavy-movement fights favor its mobility and several encounters fit its damage profile — though a couple are stinkers, with the triple-boss Lost Explorers arguably its worst fight due to lost melee uptime.

takes.creator: Pkpawner
takes.claim: He reads Shado-Pan as the stronger hero build across most of the new raid's add-heavy fights thanks to its flexible one-minute cooldowns and stomp burst for clearing add clumps, while Conduit's rigid cooldown timings force awkward delays and only shine on single-target or lineup-friendly bosses.

takes.creator: Pkpawner
takes.claim: He sees So'leah's recurring 30% damage-amp windows as a chance for Conduit Windwalker to be an exceptional pick that demolishes the boss with disciplined cooldown holding — potentially a prime Windwalker fight if spec tuning lands, reinforced by strong Detox and Transcendence utility.

takes.creator: Critcake
takes.claim: Feels the Broadstrokes nerf from twelve to six Sweeping Strikes stacks plus a Season 2 tier set with very low single-target value means Arms will feel weak after losing the strong Season 1 set and will probably need buffs.

takes.creator: Critcake
takes.claim: Is disappointed Arms rage income went unaddressed — even perfect play runs out of rage and Execute is so rage-starved that Colossus builds skip it for Slam — and argues the flat 30-second Demolish cooldown still will not sync with Colossus Smash, suggesting a two-hit combo design instead.

takes.creator: Critcake
takes.claim: Rates Fury's 12.1 direction positively: the Season 2 tier set is stronger than the current one even if boring, and together with the Hack and Slash rework should bring Raging Blow builds back for Slayer.

takes.creator: Critcake
takes.claim: Praises the Rampaging Ruin redesign for removing its single-target penalty and adding roughly four to five percent overall damage on four-target pulls in his PTR testing, making it a worthwhile Mythic+ talent point now.

takes.creator: Tettles
takes.claim: He plans to main Balance Druid again for Midnight Season 2, ideally as both raid and Mythic+ main, because 12.1's gearing changes make maintaining separate raid and M+ characters cost roughly 2.5-3 item levels.

takes.creator: Tettles
takes.claim: He says picking Augmentation Evoker for Season 1 Mythic+ was a good meta read: the spec has been very strong all season and carried him to about 4160 rating almost entirely through pugging.

takes.creator: Kesslive
takes.claim: Kesslive is a big fan of Augmentation's 12.1 two-set (Upheaval cooldown reduced by 10 seconds, stacking with Eruption-based CDR), expecting frequent quadruple Bombardments and heavy Breath of Eons cooldown reduction that he calls amazing for Mythic+.

takes.creator: Kesslive
takes.claim: He calls the reduction of duplicated Upheavals' knock-up/stopping power a fair nerf to something that was a little overpowered, and since it is the only apex-talent change so far, Augmentation's core duplication gameplay loop and power remain intact.

takes.creator: Kesslive
takes.claim: He is not excited about Devastation's 12.1 tier set at all, criticizing Blizzard for once again buffing Eternity Surge through tier bonuses instead of fixing an ability that players only cast at rank one, and notes week-one PTR Devastation is even behind Preservation on single-target.

takes.creator: Kesslive
takes.claim: He is cautiously optimistic about the rank-three apex rework that ends Dragonrage with casts of the new Unbound Flame (essentially Engulf returning), saying it finally gives the cooldown something to press but may feel clunky, particularly for Scalecommander, and he wanted a deeper rework of the spec's issues.

takes.creator: Baze
takes.claim: Celebrates Rend becoming Arms-exclusive in 12.1, saying Fury has been plagued by the ability all through Midnight and is finally free of it in single target.

takes.creator: Baze
takes.claim: Supports Demolish moving to a flat 30-second cooldown aligned with Colossus Smash since he dislikes desynced cooldowns and thinks Arms needs streamlining, calling it a step in the right direction while noting the spec still needs more work.

takes.creator: Baze
takes.claim: Argues the Hack and Slash rework misses Fury's real problem: Raging Blow simply does not do damage while Bloodthirst is too strong in Slayer and Thane builds, so Raging Blow needs a substantial baseline buff.

takes.creator: Baze
takes.claim: Finds the Fury four-piece very promising — Bloodthirst raising Recklessness crit and Raging Blow extending its duration up to six seconds — and thinks Raging Blow could genuinely return if the tuning is strong enough.

takes.creator: Gamz
takes.claim: His Wowhead guide argues that removing Mastery's interaction with damage-over-time effects significantly increases Balance's short-window AoE burst and priority-add damage, a structural change that opens up the playstyle.

takes.creator: Gamz
takes.claim: He rates Balance as a very good spec whenever cooldowns can be lined up with big AoE phases, with Elune's Chosen the better hero talent for single target and still the best overall option in Mythic+.

takes.creator: Preheat
takes.claim: Preheat welcomes Fire's roughly 5% overall single-target buff (Pyroblast up 8%, Frostfire Bolt up 5%) as part of an overdue hero-talent tuning pass, and is switching to Frostfire Fire for his raid week because several bosses without spread cleave now look viable for it.

takes.creator: Preheat
takes.claim: He notes Arcane's flat 5% buff lands on a spec whose damage-amp padding (e.g., Crown of the Cosmos' stacking amps and Touch of the Magi double-dipping) is already outsized, so its parse dominance on those fights will only grow, with Sunfury made much better.

takes.creator: Preheat
takes.claim: He argues the tuning pass does not really move the needle for realistic progression: Frost's free spread cleave matches this raid's encounter design, so it remains the default pick even though all three Mage specs now sim similarly on single-target.

takes.creator: Shadarek
takes.claim: From reviewing player logs he argues the biggest Devourer DPS losses are cast downtime and delayed Void Rays: Void Ray outranks Collapsing Star as the highest-priority button, and playing around Voidfall stacks (roughly 12% of damage including its DoT) also feeds soul and Fury generation.

takes.creator: Bicepspump
takes.claim: The recent nerfs amount to roughly a 4-5% damage decrease for Unholy, which he considers fine: the spec was S-tier in Mythic+ and has been brought in line rather than knocked out of the meta.

takes.creator: Bicepspump
takes.claim: He calls Festering Scythe becoming a self-buff one of the best changes the spec has received, since it removes the multi-GCD target-swap setup and lets you press the weak-feeling Festering Strike far less often.

takes.creator: Bicepspump
takes.claim: The new Cycle of Death talent makes Defile-based play much smoother in his view: Defile ticks refund Putrefy cooldown, so big pulls chain extra Putrefies and Magus summons, which is where most of Unholy's AoE damage comes from.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: 12.1 pushes Beast Mastery toward sustained damage: Razor Sharp (renamed from Heart of the Pack) buffs core pet damage, the redesigned Piercing Fangs adds 15% Kill Command crit damage, and Barbed Shot (+25%), Kill Command (+20%) and Cobra Shot (+20%) all get flat buffs; Wild Thrash granting a flat 10-second Beast Cleave smooths AoE upkeep, and the tier set's Barbed Shot-to-stomp-to-empowered-Cobra-Shot loop may finally bring Cobra Shot back into the rotation.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: Survival's 12.1 changes are mostly quality-of-life: Primal Surge - a talent he calls game-defining for the spec's flow - moved up from the capstone row so it is much easier to reach, the new 2-point Razor's Edge adds crit chance/crit damage scaling to Kill Command and Raptor Strike, and Lethal Barbs was adjusted so auto-attack focus generation adapts to weapon speed, aiming to end the mandatory double-dagger builds Blizzard never intended.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: Marksmanship gets yet another talent revamp after the 12.0.5 changes disappointed: damage is front-loaded with 20% buffs to Aimed Shot, Rapid Fire, Arcane Shot, Multi-Shot and Kill Shot, Explosive Shot is redesigned into repeating explosions with Ignite-style rolling damage (but feels self-contained, barely interacting with the rest of the kit), and the reworked apex talent spreads power between Rapid Fire and Aimed Shot; he reads the direction as stripping friction out of the spec, with tuning left to decide whether it is enough.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: Arcane is the big winner of the 12.1 Mage revamp: a brand-new Prismatic Bolt apex talent (procs off Arcane Salvo consumption, instantly generates four Arcane Charges, and at rank 2 guarantees Clearcasting with +30% Missiles), Arcane Pulse redesigned into a 15-second-cooldown AoE charge generator, and a second Prismatic Barrier charge for defensive parity; with the tier set angling the spec toward Sunfury and Missiles over Spellslinger, the changes 'on paper look good' and he is excited Arcane could return to the endgame spotlight - though Spellslinger's orb jank goes unaddressed.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: Fire's 12.1 direction trades burst for sustain: Pyroblast and Flamestrike damage up 15%, but Fired Up rank 3 now reduces how often Combustion occurs, so there will be fewer big burst windows; he flags that as risky since the Combustion window is most of Fire's fun and the filler rotation is dull - Blizzard may need to make the sustained rotation more interesting if they keep shaving the burst.

takes.creator: Dalaran Gaming
takes.claim: Frost is treated as the already-functional Mage spec in 12.1 and gets only light touches: a flat 4% damage buff to base abilities rather than burst tuning, while the improved-barrier rework trades the second Ice Barrier charge (Glacial Bullet no longer grants one) for 10% physical damage reduction and a bigger absorb on each barrier.

takes.creator: YoDaTV
takes.claim: The rework decouples Prot Paladin's damage from Avenging Wrath (10% instead of 20%, with Divine Guidance falling to roughly 8-10% of damage from 20-30%), but Sentinel dropping to a 12-second baseline cuts defensive-cooldown coverage from roughly 60-70% of a dungeon to 50-60%; reworked Seal of Reprisal (mobs deal 10% less via Blessed Hammer, currently bugged to reduce YOUR damage) will be mandatory once fixed and locks out Spell Warding, and the fully passive ~3% tier set fails to buff Hammer of Wrath during Wings; he ranks Paladin the close-second tank behind Vengeance DH, cautioning that its table-topping PTR-log damage is inflated by the Marrowgar's Fang trinket.

takes.creator: YoDaTV
takes.claim: He expects Vengeance DH to be the strongest tank of Season 2 but really annoying to play: survivability is unchanged since the big late-S1 buff yet completely RNG - Void Fall stacks from 35%-chance Fracture procs plus random apex Metamorphosis procs gate your Meta cooldown - and with Aldrachi still gutted you lose Reaver's Glaive and its long-range gathering, so expect aggro problems on pull with three-target Throw Glaive.

takes.creator: YoDaTV
takes.claim: Season 1's meta tank gets gutted in 12.1: Lunation goes from ~3 seconds of Lunar Beam cooldown reduction per cast (enabling near back-to-back beams) to a flat 20-second baseline reduction, killing the live Lunar Beam/Barkskin chaining playstyle and leaving Guardian to kite or survive on Incarnation alone, which high-key players already know is not enough; he would be very surprised to see Guardian stay meta - no tank since BFA (one exception) has held meta two seasons in a row - though its tier set (+5s Incarnation) is the only tank set doing anything defensive.

takes.creator: YoDaTV
takes.claim: Brewmaster should land about where it was in Season 1: Battle Flame was reverted, the tier set adds a decent bit of self-healing from fire damage, and nothing meaningful got nerfed; with Guardian Druid gutted he expects Brew to be genuinely strong in physical-damage comps, but probably not so overpowered that it gets played in magic comps.

takes.creator: YoDaTV
takes.claim: Protection Warrior changes little in 12.1: Ravager comes back more often (every ~60s instead of ~90s) but is not an important defensive button, and its PTR damage does not look good - expect Warrior to be good on fights where Spell Reflect matters and mediocre-to-average otherwise.

takes.creator: Tettles
takes.claim: On Moonkin Monthly (with Gamz and Nickdruid) he reads the long 12.1 Balance change list as 'a whole lot of nothing' - tuning shuffles like the Cosmos proc-chance nerf, Orbit Breaker -10%, and a Celestial Fire buff, with the promised damage-cooldown nerfs barely touching Balance; the real change is the tier set: the 2-piece's instant Starfall splash is only ~10% of one tick (nearly nothing on AoE), while the 4-piece eclipse pendulum (10% waning to 2% and back) is probably strong and pushes spender timing toward eclipse edges; his biggest worry is that losing the S1 shooting-stars set collapses this tier's unusually diverse raid builds back to one default.

takes.creator: VooDooSaurus
takes.claim: The 12.1 pass nerfs Devourer's cooldown-window power — Mastery: Monster Within's in-Void-Meta bonus cut 66%, Void Ray's in/out-of-meta delta shrunk, and Collapsing Star stacks ramping slower (0/20/40/60% vs the old 0/30/60/90%) plus the lost S1 tier set — but compensates with a +20% all-ability damage aura; he measures roughly an 8-10% single-target loss if you change nothing, yet expects a small net gain once you re-stat away from the now-much-weaker Mastery into Crit/Haste, and welcomes the CD-vs-steady-state rebalance (shared with Unholy DK, Windwalker, Elemental) as good design.

takes.creator: VooDooSaurus
takes.claim: He loves the new Devourer tier set: Reap gains a 20% chance to make the next Consume instant and explode a Soul Burst for AoE, and the 4-set has that Soul Burst generate 8 soul fragments plus a Moment of Craving and buff Reap damage 20% — effectively an instant ~10-soul generator that reshapes the rotation around pressing Reap far more, which he calls very strong and fun (exact proc mechanic still being reverse-engineered), and which favors the Void's Guard hero build that reaps more.

takes.creator: VooDooSaurus
takes.claim: From dummy testing and Thursday raid testing he expects Void's Guard to be the go-to Devourer hero build in Season 2 over Annihilator — it reaps more (so gains more from the tier set) and does far more out-of-meta damage, whereas Annihilator's out-of-meta damage 'falls off the earth' — though Annihilator stays decent for big sustained AoE; he cautions melee specs currently share a broken trinket that muddies tuning reads.

takes.creator: VooDooSaurus
takes.claim: With better Sims now available, he reverses his earlier build call: the best Devourer build is a Void Scar melee build that does NOT press Collapsing Star at any target count. The Mastery/Collapsing Star nerfs plus the +20% aura pushed the spec toward Crit, and Crit-scaling melee talents (Hungry Slash now triggers Burning Blades for a 35% amp while Void-scarred, Soul Forge Blades, Flamebound) now hit hard — he cites Void Surge ~14%, Hungry Slash ~10-12%, The Hunt ~8% of damage; caster/Collapsing-Star builds sim '20-25% behind' this melee build. He calls it high-skill, fun, and mobile (only Void Ray is a hard-cast).

takes.creator: VooDooSaurus
takes.claim: He explains why Collapsing Star is dead weight this build despite still being talented (only for Rolling Torment's stacks): the Apex needed to make it hit is too expensive for Void-scarred, casting it wipes ~30 stacks of Rolling Torment (a ~20% amp on leaving meta that he calls one of the spec's best talents), and Crit — which the build stacks — scales Collapsing Star worst since rank-one already makes it always crit. He says Star needs a heavy buff. The playstyle is button-dense (Reap/Call spam plus a melee combo) with frequent burst and free cleave; Soul Emulation stays core, and Void Scar plays more mobile than Annihilator at the cost of a Shift charge.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: He frames 12.1 as trading Mistweaver's Season 1 identity (Spinning Crane Kick spam, which made it the meta healer) for a single-target Rising Sun Kick game plan, and considers it a fair, more enjoyable trade — losing the Renewing Mist tier bonus roughly cancels the RSK gains — expecting Mistweaver to stay viable and near the top of the healer charts, with more players actually enjoying it.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: After playing the new tier set in Altar of Fangs he's sold on it: it grants frequent Rising Sun Kick resets (five-plus RSKs back-to-back during a Thunder Focus Tea window), and its Ancient Teachings healing carried many pulls even while he still spun — most importantly RSK finally gives Mistweaver real priority-target and boss damage/healing, long its biggest weakness.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: New talent Vital Expenditure makes Soothing Mist slam as a HoT (he suspects it's skipped in raid for mana reasons, favoring the other node), and he's intrigued by a Rushing Wind Kick Mythic+ build — but flags that the tier set currently cannot proc off Rushing Wind Kick (~200 attempts, zero procs, apparently bugged), so that build is on hold pending a fix.

takes.creator: Whispyr
takes.claim: He reads Assassination's 12.1 changes as pretty good overall: the Implacable/apex rework is fine and now fully passive (no ramp/drop), builds stay largely the same as Season 1 with minor tweaks, and Fatebound survives the Deal Fate coin-proc nerf (100% to 60%) without forcing a Deathstalker swap — his caveat is 'as long as you ignore Unstable Toxin and some Deathstalker awkwardness.'

takes.creator: Whispyr
takes.claim: He calls the new capstone Unstable Toxin undertuned: shaving 2 seconds off Envenom is a heavy downside (Envenom uptime drops from ~95% to ~65-70%, costing Rapid Injection and Inspiring Strike uptime) for only 18% Envenom damage, so it isn't worth taking in single target or cleave — its only real niche is as a giga-AoE/priority-damage talent in Mythic+.

takes.creator: Whispyr
takes.claim: The Dashing Scoundrel rework to flat energy regeneration removes its AoE scaling (it used to snowball off poison crits into huge AoE energy), turning a must-take-everywhere ~3.5% talent into a ~2.5% single-target-only one that lost about a third of its value and is no longer the AoE pick.

takes.creator: Publik
takes.claim: He's hyped about Shadow's 12.1 direction as 'stuff we've been asking for': new Shade Burst gives passive AoE exploding apparitions (currently only ~2-3% of damage and bugged to not proc off tormented-spirit or Void Apparition apparitions), Void Form now grants charges of Void Volley (removing haste breakpoints and smoothing the rotation), and a reworked Ancient Madness (stacking haste plus Void Form extension with a decaying tail) looks like the dominant choice — leaving fun and tuning as the open questions.

takes.creator: Publik
takes.claim: He flags the Archon hero tree as wildly overtuned on the PTR — the redesigned Focused Outburst (Shadow Word: Madness during Void Form unleashes a full Void Volley) is 'insanely broken,' worth more than the entire Void Weaver tree, and he fully expects it to be nerfed before ship; separately the Resonant Energy rework (now a can't-miss flat player buff instead of a Halo-hit debuff) works around Halo's radius/dead-zone bugs for roughly a 1% Archon gain.

takes.creator: Publik
takes.claim: On the not-yet-testable tier set (2-set: Tentacle Slam cooldown -3s and damage +100%; 4-set: post-Tentacle-Slam instant Vampiric Touch that conjures 200%-damage apparitions) he's lukewarm — it leans hard into Tentacle-Slam-as-a-damage-button, which he wouldn't have chosen, and he shares the mixed community reaction especially to the 4-set, wanting something more unified; verdict pending hands-on testing.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: Voidweaver Disc — gutted and 'laid to rest' last season for its early-Season-1 raid crimes — is being brought back in a big way for Season 2. The headline change is Void Infusion buffed from 50% to 75%, so the build now wants to extend Entropic Rift as long as possible to keep that shadow-damage buff up near-permanently. He's more excited for Voidweaver than any other healer bar the Resto Shaman tier set, and in-key it out-healed the tank and posted strong atonement-driven damage — though he stresses healer damage across the board is a joke now that Spinning Crane Kick is nerfed.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: The new Disc tier set creates a very strong Voidweaver loop: Penance shaves time off the Mind Blast cooldown, so you almost always have an Entropic Rift available on demand — press Mind Blast a couple seconds before incoming party damage so the rift is grown/blown at the moment you need it, then Penance again to reset it for near-permanent rift uptime. He flags this as something to watch especially if Voidweaver starts being played in raid again, since a near-always-up healing ramp is exactly the kind of raid-oppressive pattern that gets Disc nerfed.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: Shadow Mend has been reworked from a fished-for proc (which last season could hit like a Lay on Hands) into a constant baseline effect that is individually weaker. He likes having one less proc to wait on, but worries it no longer provides the big single-target emergency punch and reads as better in raid than in M+. Separately he notes the Oracle hero tree is changing so Penance no longer applies Atonement, costing an extra global at certain times — a real but modest downside — and argues Disc needs a defining gimmick (like the perma-uptime rift ramp) rather than raw numbers, because whenever it's numerically strong it breaks raid and then gets nerfed into the ground for M+.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: The headline 20% blanket damage buff sounds big but he calls it 'almost nothing' in practice — Holy Paladin's damage baseline is so low (roughly 7k DPS even while burning every offensive global, wings and Divine Toll) that 20% of it barely moves the needle. The meaningful damage gain is actually the buffs to the single-target spenders Word of Glory and Eternal Flame, which through Liberation make a single-target-spender build viable again: he drops the three Light of Dawn talent points, presses Judgment on cooldown for Righteous Judgment, and leans on Shield of the Righteous → Shining Righteousness → Divine Purpose for free enhanced spenders. He still wants more, e.g. Righteous Judgment buffed toward Healing Rain's damage.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: He didn't think Holy Paladin needed the healing buffs — it has been a very good healer two seasons running, just slept-on and overshadowed by Mistweaver — and with Mistweaver's Spinning Crane Kick nerfed he thinks the door is open for Holy Paladin to 'take the throne if it wants it.' He praises its high skill ceiling and high floor (harder to pilot than press-SCK Mistweaver), notes comfortable mana even without Beacon of Virtue on this single-target-spender build, and expects a competitive healer season overall — while cautioning the damage still needs another pass and that he doubts Blizzard is done tweaking the spec.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: The Restoration tier set is the 12.1 change he's most excited about across all healers. The 2-piece gives Healing Wave and Chain Heal a chance to spawn a free Healing Rain on the target, so because Resto spams those buttons you get a near-constant stream of extra Healing Rains — it topped his healing sources in a key even while he mostly spammed Healing Wave. He flags a working PTR interaction where Acid Rain damage and Downpour both apply to those free tier-set Healing Rains — an emergent 'step-two' buff that quietly strengthens Downpour and Acid Rain builds even though the patch notes don't list them as changed.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: He's lukewarm on the 4-piece: it rolls a shield onto one person standing in your Healing Rain at a time, which he finds boring and 'non-exciting', and — more importantly — he doubts it scales into raid, since one-in-five people getting a shield in a 5-man is far more valuable than one-in-twenty in a raid. He worries a set this strong in M+ won't translate to the raid healing Resto actually needs.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: Despite getting one of the shortest change lists of any healer, Resto is a net gainer in 12.1 (unlike Mistweaver, which he sees losing ground to the Spinning Crane Kick nerf). The Farseer hero tree is buffed fairly significantly — ancestor damage up, Chain Lightning hitting more targets, and Lava Burst scaling harder with crit — on top of a quality-of-life change letting Farseer reposition its Healing Rain. He's impressed with the Farseer tree and keen to play it.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: Bottom line: a genuinely fun, high-variance tier set ('you gamble, but if you win, you win big') and he's a fan of the spec — but he still thinks Resto needs more raid-healing love and doesn't expect much from it in raid testing, since the standout 2-piece is weighted toward 5-man healing and doesn't scale up; he'd even settle for a flat Healing Rain healing buff. He also notes Resto does noticeably less spec damage than Mistweaver, especially off Farseer in single target.

takes.creator: AutomaticJak
takes.claim: In a Pres-mains panel (Kigalion + Youtee), the 12.1 Dream Breath rework is a clear win: instant healing cut and periodic healing raised so Dream Breath becomes a much bigger HoT, and Maritha’s Blessing now amplifies all healing rather than only instant heals. The empower cast finally feels meaningful again — in raid testing Dream Breath was ~35–40% of total HPS. Flame Shaper (two Dream Breath charges, plus Dormo’s Teaching CDR off Temporal Anomaly, sustainable on its mana) is the favored build to spam it, and picks up a rot-fight niche while Chrono Warden stays the burst option.

takes.creator: AutomaticJak
takes.claim: They rate the Season 2 tier set 2-piece as “one of the stronger ones we’ve ever had”: consuming Essence Burst fires a 150%-effectiveness Living Flame that can itself proc more Essence Burst, chaining into very wide ramps (six-to-eight Essence Bursts in a row) without leaning entirely on Temporal Anomaly — it felt “phenomenal” in raid testing. The 4-piece (5% more green healing + Verdant Embrace guaranteeing Essence Burst) is treated as a minor, rarely-cast bonus — the 2-piece is the whole story.

takes.creator: AutomaticJak
takes.claim: A flagged (and admittedly controversial) upside in M+: the 2-piece Living Flame loop plus Disintegrate procs lets Preservation put out ~100k single-target damage in current PTR keys — “more than most tanks” — while still weaving healing globals. They frame real personal throughput as essentially required for a non-raid-buff healer to compete, comparing it to Mistweaver’s personal damage.

takes.creator: AutomaticJak
takes.claim: Positioning/watch: they read Pres as balanced-not-busted (“you don’t get brought when you’re balanced, you get brought when you’re busted”), carried by an elite prog niche — snapping the whole raid’s health up after mechanics. But bigger 12.1 HP pools blunt burst healing, and Midnight M+ heal checks are punishing (e.g. the new dungeon chieftain’s Nexus-Point-tier heal absorb on a +12), so any flat aura nerf could push Pres out of M+ viability. They’re asking for M+-specific tuning (stronger echoes or a bigger Maritha’s Blessing DR in keys) rather than blanket numbers.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: In 12.1 PTR M+ testing he came in a self-described 'hater' of the Season 2 Preservation tier set — consuming an Essence Burst sends a free Living Flame at 1.5x effectiveness at your target or a nearby injured ally — doubting its raid longevity, but came away 'thoroughly impressed.' The Essence-Burst-fueled Echo spot heals 'chunk' (he eyeballs ~35% of a health bar), and because Verdant Embrace has a 100% chance to grant an Essence Burst, procs come up constantly, giving ample resource to either apply Echo for a burst spot heal or fire Disintegrate into an enemy (which also throws the tier-set Living Flame for extra damage). Final verdict: the set is 'actually really good, a lot better than I thought.' Corroborates AutomaticJak's read that the 2-piece is strong.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: Notes the 12.1 change shifts Dream Breath's power out of its big instant burst-heal and into its HoT ('they really juiced up the HoT, it ticks pretty hard now'), which changes usage — he is re-learning not to aggressively recast Dream Breath purely for the initial burst when the HoT is already rolling, and wonders whether doubling up Temporal Anomaly for two rolling Dream Breath HoTs becomes preferable. Also flags Nazdormu's/Dormo's Teachings (Temporal Anomaly shaving 5s off empowered-spell cooldowns) as keeping the rotation flowing. Complements AutomaticJak's Dream Breath rework take.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.claim: Reads Preservation as still fun and 'slept on' going into Season 2 (as it was in S1, where the build wasn't solved until a mid-season rework) rather than an obvious meta pick, and cautions it's 'way too early to tell' which healer ends up best. He plays Chrono Warden but thinks the 12.1 changes make Flame Shaper 'look pretty solid' and wants to test it, likes double Verdant Embrace even more in S2, and floats an experimental Emerald Blossom Essence-Burst-spender build as possible 'spice.'

takes.creator: Critcake
takes.claim: The Arms Colossus bleed build is back on the 12.1 PTR: the Season 2 tier set has a bleed-focused bonus and Ravager was reworked to buff bleed damage. The rotation shifts substantially — Cleave stays top priority and now applies Rend to every target it hits (so Rend is only pressed manually on single target), and he thinks Arterial Bleed (bleeds deal more damage per Colossal Might stack) is now likely worth taking over the Season 1 pick One Against Many.

takes.creator: Critcake
takes.claim: On how the bleed build plays in Season 2 keys: he prioritizes Mortal Strike until 10 Colossal Might stacks, then Slam over Mortal Strike because the tier set makes Slam apply AoE Deep Wounds. Spamming Slam in AoE feels strange — he calls it stupid-feeling and has to actively concentrate without the muscle memory — but he concedes it works, with Deep Wounds topping his damage breakdown in the dungeon runs.

takes.creator: Critcake
takes.claim: His broader Season 2 PTR read on Arms: pure single-target output is nothing impressive but feels less bad than in the current season. He thinks 12.1 is still a funnel-oriented season — funnel is slightly less effective than before but still clearly worth doing to speed keys up. One sore spot: Sweeping Strikes uptime is noticeably lower than in Season 1, which feels bad.

takes.creator: AutomaticJak
takes.claim: The expected Dream Breath nerfs landed in build 68569: Inner Flame's periodic healing bonus drops from 60% to 50%, Spiritual Clarity's cooldown reduction goes from 10 to 6 seconds, and Flame Shaper takes hits to Expanded Lungs (30% to 20%), Conduit of Flames crit (15% to 10%), and Fulminous Roar. He thinks the Conduit change also cuts Essence Burst generation, which fed the tier set's high-powered Living Flames, so M+ is hurt most; Preservation should still be very strong in raid since it was miles ahead of other healers in the first Heroic testing wave, though the lack of a raid buff remains a killer in dungeons.

takes.creator: AutomaticJak
takes.claim: The Temple Training change is essentially net neutral: it was unintentionally boosting Mistweaver's Spinning Crane Kick, so Blizzard raised base damage and removed the interaction. His headline is that spinning is still really powerful — with Dance of Chi-Ji it does a ton of healing and powers through heavy damage — and single-target kicks plus the Rising Sun Kick tier set are also very strong. He argues the mindless spin-to-win dungeon playstyle should go in favor of more reliable Dance procs, but after testing five dungeons Mistweaver remains extremely strong.

takes.creator: AutomaticJak
takes.claim: Holy Paladin got a broad buff package in build 68569: all healing up 8%, Word of Glory/Eternal Flame up 5%, Holy Shock up 10%, a much stronger Infusion of Light, plus buffs to Truth Prevails, Overflowing Light (30% to 50%), Awakening, and Tyr's Devotion. He highlights a no-Holy-Shock build (credited to Clarius) built on Judgment and 4-set Holy Light forcing Infusion procs spent on Flash of Light — fun but degenerate, since Holy Shock stays weak even after its buff. His broader read: positive changes, but the spec's chunky direct-healing kit is punished by the rot-damage raid meta and Avenging Wrath remains a weak cooldown, so plenty of problems remain.

takes.creator: AutomaticJak
takes.claim: He's very happy with Disc's build-68569 tuning package, which deliberately shifts power away from Void Shield (absorb nerfed 30% after doing 40-50% of total healing) into the rest of the kit: all healing/absorbs up 10%, Atonement up from 30% to 32% of damage done — a long-overdue Atonement buff — Ultimate Penitence up 30%, and Greater Smite's buff duration doubled to 4 seconds so Voidweaver can weave Penance and Mind Blast without dropping stacks. He thinks it's the right direction (maybe even more power should move into Atonement), and with Void Shield tamed, Voidweaver now has a real chance over Oracle in keys — though mana remains a problem.

takes.creator: AutomaticJak
takes.claim: Holy Priest gets all healing up 10%, Benediction's mana cost cut 30%, and Apotheosis' Holy Word mana discount raised from 50% to 70%, but he doesn't think this fixes the core issue: the spec's strong direct casts are punished by the current rot-damage raid design, where passive-HoT healers consume the damage before chunky casts land. In his Heroic testing he could keep pace only briefly before being outpaced, and the spec lacks the M+ mana efficiency Monk, Druid, and Shaman enjoy — so Holy Priest stays on the struggle bus despite the buffs.

takes.creator: Kalamazi
takes.claim: He argues the new 2-set nerf (Seed of Corruption now applying Unstable Affliction at 20% effectiveness, down from 50%) is unwarranted and likely driven by unfixed bugs: since day one of the PTR, seed-applied UA has been ticking at full 100% value instead of the intended 50%, and separately a one-stack seed-applied UA deals no damage at all. His own math says a properly-working 50% would already make seeding slightly worse than hard-casting UA in single target, so inflated data misled the tuning. He wants the change reverted and the bugs fixed instead — at 20% the applied UA ticks for under 1k while Corruption hits for roughly four times that.

takes.creator: Kalamazi
takes.claim: The 20% change effectively kills the tier set's funnel identity — once the 100%-value bug is fixed it amounts to roughly an 80% nerf to seed-applied UA, leaving the 4-set as little more than a passive damage amp. That matters because several upcoming raid fights (Nexali, Sentinels, Vashnik, Twin Fangs, Lost Explorers) have priority-target-plus-adds profiles where the seed-funnel setup was Affliction's niche; strip that and other specs simply do the stacked-cleave job better, benching Affliction. He'd rather see the bonus reworked outright than shipped in this dead state.

takes.creator: Kalamazi
takes.claim: He calls the 10% Destruction aura buff very large but deserved — the spec was behind by a significant margin, only okay on a few raid fights like Twin Fangs and the Tomb Sentinels. Post-buff he thinks Destruction (especially Diabolist builds) could be playable on several raid fights this tier thanks to its cleave, though he stops short of calling it a full meta pick yet.

takes.creator: Kalamazi
takes.claim: He's strongly critical of the earlier Hellcaller change that removed Blackened Soul's ability to gain stacks from Rain of Fire and Seed of Corruption: it removes the spender that fuels the entire hero talent, which he says fundamentally breaks how the spec flows and makes Hellcaller unplayable in Mythic+ (probably still okay on a few raid fights). He understands the stated goal of better priority-target funnel damage but says the implementation doesn't function, and he hopes the change is reverted.

takes.creator: YoDaTV
takes.claim: The latest 12.1 PTR build completely fixed the Protection Paladin talent tree in his view: Spell Warding is no longer stuck in a choice node with Improved Ardent Defender, previously blocking talents are now choice nodes, and Sanctuary and Relentless Inquisitor moved up so the bottom gate is no longer a problem — you can keep your old point spread. He notes the new damage-vs-Sanctuary choice node is a real pick in raid/low keys but 5% DR still wins in serious keys.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.patchContext: 12.1 PTR — build 68569 healer tuning (dev notes reaction)
takes.claim: Reads the Genesis nerf (healing-over-time amp 25% → 15%) as an expected, healthy redistribution — the proc was so strong it 'kneecapped' how strong the rest of the kit could be — with the compensating mastery buff enabling better tank/spot healing in dungeons and better raid healing; also likes the 4-set redesign (Nature's Swiftness/Tranquility/Ironbark now 100% chance to grant a longer Genesis): less oomph per proc, more uptime. Overall 'great tweaks in the right direction' to keep Resto Druid stable into Season 2.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.patchContext: 12.1 PTR — build 68569 healer tuning (dev notes reaction)
takes.claim: Sees the wave of Dream Breath support-talent nerfs (Inner Flame 60% → 50%, Spiritual Clarity CDR 10s → 6s, Expanded Lungs 30% → 20%, Fulminous Roar 20% → 15%) as justified rebalancing after the Dream Breath HoT was 'giga buffed' — 'the natural flow of tuning'; highlights the Consume Flame buff (200% → 300% of consumed HoT) plus new Fluttering Seedlings consumption and injured-ally targeting preference as real gains for a Flame Shaper/Emerald Blossom build. Preservation 'still looking pretty solid' on the PTR; he wants more Flame Shaper reps before a verdict.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.patchContext: 12.1 PTR — build 68569 healer tuning (dev notes reaction)
takes.claim: Reads the Spinning Crane Kick buff as a deliberate partial walk-back of the overcorrected pre-PTR nerf, not a re-break: Mistweaver is already 'performing the best in a key on the PTR', and this just makes SCK feel justified again on big AoE packs while keeping the healthier press-all-your-buttons play. People scared of 'why buff Mistweaver?' are misreading a self-correction.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.patchContext: 12.1 PTR — build 68569 healer tuning (dev notes reaction)
takes.claim: The change list he's most excited about in the whole healer wave: +8% all healing, the 2-set Infusion of Light effectiveness 75% → 100%, Word of Glory/Eternal Flame +5%, and Holy Shock +10% (which cascades into Divine Toll and every Holy-Shock-scaling talent). Calls Overflowing Light (30% → 50% of Holy Shock overhealing to shield) a 'double whammy' with the Holy Shock buff and the change that excites him most — he sees a potential shielding build and Avenging Crusader/Lightsmith becoming at least conversational, though nothing here 'busts or breaks' Holy Paladin.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.patchContext: 12.1 PTR — build 68569 healer tuning (dev notes reaction)
takes.claim: Reads the Void Shield −30% absorption vs Ultimate Penitence +30% / Greater Smite 4s / blanket +10% healing-absorption package as a real trade-off — stronger cooldown moments, weaker steady-state, with Oracle likely feeling the Void Shield nerf hardest in M+. His critique: Blizzard keeps toggling the Atonement slider when they should leave Atonement alone and buff Penance DAMAGE instead — on the current PTR a Voidweaver Penance 'kind of tickles' where it used to be the capitalizing ramp finisher.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.patchContext: 12.1 PTR — build 68569 healer tuning (dev notes reaction)
takes.claim: The +10% healing buff was 'definitely necessary' for a spec reliant on healing alone, and the mana help (Benediction cost reduction, Apotheosis holy-word discount 50% → 70%) targets the real Archon-build mana strain — but of the whole healer wave he is 'least excited about the lack of things on Holy Priest'; he's saving a fuller verdict for a dedicated healer-opinions video.

takes.creator: LBNinja7
takes.patchContext: 12.1 PTR — build 68569 healer tuning (dev notes reaction)
takes.claim: Resto Shaman 'finally makes it on a patch note and it feels like three quality-of-life changes': Acid Rain +50% damage but ticking every 2s (was 1s) — front-loaded value whose intent he can't read (guaranteed first tick vs a stealth raid-movement nerf) — plus two Healing Rain tick/partial-tick bug fixes that matter more now the tier set spawns extra Healing Rains. He's genuinely ambivalent whether the package is a positive or a negative.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Frost DK as the clear standout of the raid testing — the one outlier that sits at or near the top of essentially every boss regardless of whether the fight wants single target, cleave or AoE. He credits its free passive AoE (Icy Death Torrent auto-attack cleave) for letting it excel on single-target fights without giving anything up, and names it the overall best DPS so far.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen puts Frost Mage among the top three overall DPS in the testing, driven mainly by its strong two-target cleave, which shone on the cleave-heavy fights like Twin Fangs and the Lost Explorers council.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen rates Shadow Priest as one of the best overall performers, valued for its multi-target/multi-dot flexibility — it held up on the caster AoE opener, was the biggest non-Frost-DK outlier on the two-target Tomb Sentinels, and reached five-target spread cleave on Twin Fangs.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen highlights Arms Warrior as a strong overall performer thanks to its very good two-target cleave from Sweeping Strikes, which gave it value on the council and Twin Fangs fights, and it also placed among the top melee on the movement-heavy single-target boss.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen sees Affliction as a strong AoE/multi-dot pick that performed well on caster-and-spread fights — the add-heavy opener, the council (agony/corruption across three targets), and Twin Fangs' five-target spread. He notes it's less ideal on pure single target, since it has to actively cast for its AoE rather than getting it for free, but overall counts it among the better specs.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen counts BM Hunter among the strong overall performers and calls it the 'melee-adopted' ranged spec — fully mobile with instant abilities, so it topped the movement-heavy pure-single-target fight (Zoroak) alongside the melee where static casters struggled.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen lists Havoc among the better overall DPS of the testing, and it appeared near the top of the movement-heavy single-target fight where mobile melee were favored.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen says Arcane Mage was doing very well on the caster-friendly fights — the sustained-AoE opener and the stationary single-target boss (Bashnik) — because it picks up free AoE through Arcane Missiles/Barrage and the prismatic-bolt splash while doing its single-target rotation.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen names Balance among the casters favored on the sustained-AoE opener (Nexali) and includes it in the five-target spread-cleave group that thrives on Twin Fangs, so he reads it as well-positioned on the multi-target fights.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen lists Elemental among the casters doing well on the large sustained-AoE opener (Nexali), where slow ramping multi-target damage is rewarded.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen calls Preservation Evoker the clear best healer in the testing — practically the only spec pushing toward ~300k HPS. He credits the big Dream Breath heal-over-time buff (average ~1.2M per HoT cast) plus the new tier set's Sea of Essence bursts, which have pushed it into a Flame Shaper build.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Discipline as essentially unchanged — still an atonement spec, with Blizzard continuing to shift power toward Void Shield and away from atonement, a trend that isn't reversing in the patch. He frames it as static rather than improving.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen sees Mistweaver as unchanged — same very high APM, same three-ability rotation cadence, and the same hero-talent/talent setup, with no meaningful shift in how it plays or performs.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen groups Holy Priest with the healers that were performing horribly in the testing; it has only been buffed 'a bit,' leaving it well behind the standout Preservation.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen counts Holy Paladin among the healers that were doing badly in the raid testing, receiving only a small buff so far and still lagging the top healers.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Frost DK as looking strong for S2 M+ — it is far and away the most-played DPS in the PTR. He credits the rework: the burst window (Pillar of Frost, Breath, Obliterate) was nerfed while passive damage like Howling Blast, Icy Death Torrent and Remorseless Winter was roughly doubled, and it is putting up top results. He cautions the heavy play partly reflects excitement over big changes, but the parses back it up.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Unholy as staying meta and strong into S2, but sees it as the less exciting DK — its PTR changes look long on paper yet barely alter the playstyle, so fewer players are testing it versus the more heavily reworked Frost.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Arcane as looking strong and among the most-played DPS in PTR M+. He points to an Apex rework adding Prismatic Bolt (now the hardest-hitting ability) and a new tier set that pulls Arcane Missiles back into the core AoE rotation, driving both strong results and player hype.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Fire Mage as weak — he flatly says Fire and Frost are 'down there because they're bad, especially Fire Mage,' which is why Mage interest has shifted entirely to Arcane.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Frost Mage as bad/weak for S2, lumping it with Fire as the underperforming Mage specs players are abandoning in favor of the reworked Arcane.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen notes Guardian is the reigning meta tank yet the least-played tank in the PTR — not from any tuning knock, but because it is overshadowed by the newly buffed Prot Paladin soaking up all the interest.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Prot Paladin as the standout tank for S2, by far the most-played, thanks to a large pile of defensive buffs: Ardent Defender granting +20% max HP and now keeping its buffs through the cheat-death proc, the now-fixed Silver Reprisal, plus more self-healing from Avenger's Shield, Consecration and a much-improved Word of Glory.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Aug as still strong (hence still popular) but not improved — it gets almost no tuning, loses a powerful S1 tier set for roughly a wash, and takes an M+ nerf now that its Apex no longer grants free knock-up crowd control. Nothing to be excited about.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Marksmanship as trending up — after its latest changes (including repeated Explosive Shot buffs) it has climbed in both play rate and results, doing very well especially in AoE, and is now a genuine alternative to BM.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads BM as looking good — it was already the stronger Hunter spec by default, and its tier set makes Cobra Shot (an already-frequent filler) its strongest button, without changing the rotation.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Affliction as the dominant, most-played Warlock in PTR M+ results and top damage — but he is puzzled, because it is one of the least-changed specs (only minor tier-set buffs to Agony/Corruption), so its strength is not really coming from S2 tuning.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Havoc as looking good, holding near-equal damage and popularity with Devourer despite receiving little tuning focus — its 4-piece tier set brings back Essence Break, a ~40s burst window that lines up perfectly with M+ pulls.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Devourer as looking good for S2, sitting near-equal to Havoc in both damage and popularity — it got the biggest rework, with a large mastery/in-meta damage nerf offset by a flat 20% buff to everything else plus a 'very cool' new tier set.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Mistweaver as remaining one of the two most-popular healers, carrying over its established S1 strength with nothing notable changed.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Disc as a top-popularity healer for S2, played into far more and much higher keys than a struggling spec like Preservation.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen lists Resto Shaman among the still-popular, 'still here' healers alongside Mistweaver and Disc, but gives it no individual read — essentially unchanged from S1.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: izen reads Preservation as improving but still weak in M+ — historically poor and still stuck in few, low keys far behind other healers, though it gets another round of helpful changes (low-target healing QOL, plus a tier set granting extra Essence Bursts and a 150% Chrono Flame). Nowhere near the hype of Prot Pal, Frost DK or Arcane.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Reads Frost DK as the dominant PTR performer — showing up constantly in top M+ keys and strong in raid, the best of all melee alongside Unholy — and calls its huge nerf (he estimates roughly 20% single-target, ~17% AoE effective) the most deserved of the round.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Warns not to assume Unholy was weak just because Frost stood out: in raid testing Unholy was the spec appearing most in single-target and 2-3 target results (especially the season's pure single-target boss), among the best melee — hence a roughly 15% nerf hitting its core pet damage.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Sees Havoc as having been doing okay on the PTR — clearly better than Devourer but not at the nerfed DKs' level — and finds its 5% nerf a bit surprising.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Says Devourer's rework direction was praised but its actual damage was low — effectively nerfed compared to live — so the 10% buff is Blizzard trying to bump an underperformer up.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Reads Balance as well positioned for this raid: it wasn't awful in single target, the tested raid has few pure single-target fights, and Balance benefits from multi-target on many encounters — with the new buffs skewing AoE, he calls it a good change even if parts net out flat.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Judges the Scalecommander package to be in a pretty tough spot for Devastation after the Wingleader rework — decoupling the cooldown reduction from Bombardments is both a playstyle change and a significant net nerf in his view.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Extends the same read to Augmentation: the Wingleader rework leaves Scalecommander in a tough situation for both Evoker DPS specs, since the cooldown reduction is far less frequent than before.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Calls Preservation the best-performing healer in PTR raid testing by a reasonable margin — essentially the only healer clearing the top HPS benchmarks — driven by the Dream Breath HoT rebalance, so the broad nerf to that package was expected.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Reads BM's changes as roughly a 10%+ net nerf that lands badly: BM was doing decently in single target but quite poorly in AoE, and the round guts its single-target strengths (Cobra Shot package, tier set halved) while giving nothing for the weak AoE.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Says Marksmanship wasn't performing nearly as well as BM, particularly in single target, and calls its broad ~8-10% buff (everything except Aimed/Explosive Shot) quite good for the spec.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Reads Fire as doing extremely low damage across the board on the PTR and being barely played in testing — the ~12% buff is Blizzard trying to revive a spec players weren't even bothering to try.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Considers Windwalker to be doing okay damage-wise — not in a poor spot — but as traditionally one of the squishiest specs, he sees the stamina and damage-reduction buffs as a needed defensive fix rather than a throughput change.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Calls Holy Paladin one of the biggest winners — a second consecutive round of large buffs to spenders, all healing, and the tier set — though he flags a design worry that the buffed, infusion-empowered single-target spender is now so strong it threatens to make Holy Shock obsolete until a follow-up rebalance.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Flags Protection Paladin as a potential meta pick: it kept its double round of buffs without a nerf, and PTR M+ tanks already pushing 18-20 keys are doing it on Prot Paladin — a strong hint about its power level.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Reads Holy Priest as one of the few healers failing the raid HPS benchmarks, with severe mana problems; the mana discounts and 10% healing buff help but skip its biggest mana drain, moving it from terrible to only slightly below average in his estimation.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Sees Shadow as already doing well — a major beneficiary of the many multi-target fights in this raid — and finds it curious that it's getting a further flat 8% buff anyway, though he notes it wasn't especially popular as a DPS pick.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Says the 8% Subtlety nerf makes no sense to him: Sub was not doing great in raid testing and was unpopular in M+, so unless Blizzard has a bigger rework planned, the spec is just left worse off.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Reads Affliction as having been around top-5 M+ damage, but largely via a degenerate tier-set interaction that let players skip casting Unstable Affliction entirely; the effectiveness cut from 50% to 20% closes that loophole and forces the real rotation back.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Calls Destruction the worst-performing spec alongside Fire Mage — very poor damage and very little play in testing — with the flat 10% buff as its only lifeline.

metaNotes.creator: izen (Izenhart)
metaNotes.note: Reads Arms as notably weak in pure single-target raid fights while doing quite a bit better with cleave and in M+ AoE; the new buffs (execute, melee damage) deliberately target that single-target hole, worth roughly 5% ST and almost nothing in AoE.
